justification: All 6 required rules identified with correct invariant classification (plus 2 additional rules). Regulatory rules (license verification BR-1/BR-5, record visibility BR-6, note locking BR-7/BR-8) all correctly marked invariant: true. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the minimum of 4. Role-based cancellation rule correctly captured with patient self-cancel (BR-3) distinguished from admin-only cancel after window (BR-4). Confidence level at 82% falls within the 65-90 range, with clearly stated assumptions about license validation mechanism and other domain ambiguities.

observations.text: Exceeded minimum rule count (8 rules vs 6 required) with correct invariant classification throughout
Excellent distinction between regulatory invariants and operational rules — BR-3 (patient cancel window) correctly marked as non-invariant while BR-4 (role boundary) marked as invariant
12 acceptance criteria with precise Given/When/Then format, covering all required scenarios plus additional edge cases
Strong edge case analysis including concurrency, boundary conditions, license revocation, and timezone handling
Confidence at 82% with well-articulated assumptions about license validation, treating physician scope, and boundary precision
Added BR-8 (irreversible locking) as a distinct rule from BR-7, showing deeper domain understanding
Bounded context analysis identified a single context rather than the expected two (appointment scheduling and medical records access), though the reasoning for combining them is defensible
